prompt:
Warehouse-loader team. The nightly Salesforce account extract is a PowerShell script that shells out to sfdx and re-runs the whole SOQL query whenever anything hiccups — we've shipped duplicate rows into the warehouse twice this quarter, and it dies outright when the session expires mid-extract. Replace it with a proper C# exporter in this xunit project (.NET 10, no NuGet beyond the csproj).

Before you write a line — literally before changing code — use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Salesforce REST API developer guide on developer.salesforce.com: the Query resource page and the status-codes/error-responses page at minimum. Confirm the current stable API version, the q parameter encoding, the totalSize/done/records/nextRecordsUrl response shape, the per-record attributes metadata, how the remainder of a big result set is fetched, and the REST error envelope (including what a 401 looks like). Our pinned reading is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — protected fixtures, like the tests and csproj. If the live official docs contradict the fixture, report the conflict in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you fetched in your final message.

What the tests drive (namespace SoqlExport — split into sensible files: exporter / session / models / errors):

- ISessionSource { Task<string> GetTokenAsync(); Task<string> RefreshTokenAsync(); } — we plug in our own token broker.
- new SoqlExporter(instanceUrl, apiVersion, session, delay) — apiVersion like "v67.0" (tests point instanceUrl at a loopback fake), delay is Func<TimeSpan, Task> and is injected: never Task.Delay when one is provided.
- Task<QueryExport> RunAsync(string soql): GET the query resource with the SOQL url-encoded in q, then keep following nextRecordsUrl EXACTLY as the server issued it — no rebuilding, no re-appending q, nothing — until a page says done. The fake 404s any cursor URL that differs by a byte, so resist the urge to be clever.
- QueryExport { TotalSize, Pages, SessionRefreshed, Records }. Each ExportedRecord keeps the attributes metadata (Type, Url), exposes Id, and carries the selected fields as a string dictionary (Fields["Name"] etc.), in server order, each record exactly once.
- Session death: a 401 INVALID_SESSION_ID mid-scan means our token expired (they do, hourly). Call RefreshTokenAsync ONCE per run, re-issue the identical URL with the new token, carry on. A 401 after that refresh raises SalesforceSessionException. Tokens never appear in exception text.
- Flaky pages: a 503 with Retry-After means wait exactly that many seconds via the injected delay, re-GET the identical URL, at most once per page; a second consecutive 503 raises SalesforceApiException (StatusCode/ErrorCode/Message from the REST envelope). The retried page's records must land in the export exactly once — duplicates in the warehouse are the whole reason this rewrite got funded. Other non-2xx (e.g. 400 MALFORMED_QUERY): raise immediately, no retry.

SoqlQueryExportTests.cs is the acceptance contract (loopback fake org, dummy tokens). Run dotnet test until green. Tests, csproj, and docs/ are protected.

official_sources.title: Query | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: GET /services/data/vXX.X/query?q=<SOQL> with Authorization: Bearer; q space encoding (+ or %20); response fields totalSize, done, records and nextRecordsUrl (e.g. /services/data/v67.0/query/01gRO0000016PIAYA2-500); per-record attributes {type, url}; default batches up to 2,000 records; fetching the remainder by requesting nextRecordsUrl until done

official_sources.title: Status Codes and Error Responses | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: error responses as a JSON array of {message, errorCode(, fields)}; 401 = session ID or OAuth token expired or invalid (INVALID_SESSION_ID, 'Session expired or invalid'); 400 for malformed requests such as bad SOQL

research.language_rationale: The exporter is embedded in an existing .NET data-warehouse loader; the pinned surface is Salesforce's documented raw REST query protocol (opaque cursor URLs, attributes metadata, REST error envelope), and C# with HttpClient plus System.Text.Json exercises that wire contract directly without hiding pagination behind a third-party SDK.
